series_title: Employee Early Retirement Files and Reports

series_description: ﻿Direct deposit warrant transactions that have failed due to on-line entry of incorrect or incomplete account information from claimants who have elected to have their UC benefits directly deposited into their bank account. Benefits Finance issues a Notice of Eligibility Issue form alerting the claimant of the error and requesting they send in a voided personal check to verify the correct account information. Once the correct data has been entered into the Ohio Job Insurance (OJI) system, the voided checks are put into a locked pouch and sent to Records Management for shredding. The pouch is then returned to Benefits Finance for future use.
retention_statement: Electronic: ﻿Retain until audited by Auditor of State, report is released and all discrepancies are resolved ; Paper: ﻿Retain in office until information is entered into OJI

series_description: Registry includes items such as name, address, unemployment insurance serial number, North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code, and county code. Information is used to create address labels and to pick-up processing codes needed to process data for estimating purposes.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office until superseded, obsolete or replaced

series_description: Record of nominations and awards recognizing individuals or teams or outstanding service to ODJFS. Files include nominations, selections, charter, program details.

series_title: Precedent Setting Decisions involving Lower & Higher Authority Appeals, & Court Appeals
series_description: Decisions on all appeals of cases coming to the U.C. Review Commission. Includes decisions on cases involving any unemployment compensation appeal, further appeal, tax liability, federal program, mass or labor dispute, delinquent account, employer contribution, or from the Courts. Precedent setting decisions are a compilation of decisions which illustrate precedent in new and unusual cases and decisions which serve as examples of frequently encountered situations.
retention_statement: Electronic: All decisions are to be stored permanently per 4141.06 of the ORC. It requires the Secretary of the UCRC to keep a record of all decisions because of periodic requests from employers and other interested parties.

series_description: List of lower and higher authority decisions mailed to all interested parties on a given day. This list certifies that on the date and time shown on the list, decisions of the U.C. Board of Review were enclosed in envelopes bearing the interested party''s names and addresses and submitted to the mailroom for affixing of proper postage and deposited in the mail on the date shown on the certified list. Record of all Decisions mailed. Includes a daily listing of: appeal docket number; claimants name, employers, and addresses; total number of cases, and total number of actual mailings; signatures of Commission representative and ODJFS Mail Services representative.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 2 years and in records center 3 years

series_title: Court - Open and Closed Case Files and UC Review Commission Hearing Recordings
series_description: Appeals from Higher Authority decisions. The case file may contain the following: notice of appeal to court, copy of appellants appeal, demand for transcript, copy of the Director''s file, transcript of Hearing Officer and Commission hearings, decisions of the hearing officer and the Commission, application to institute further appeal, notice of hearings, correspondence relevant to the case, decisions of the courts, and cassette audio tape recording of the hearing officer and Commission hearings.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 2 years after the case is closed and in records center 3 years ; Electronic: Retain in office 5 years after the case is closed

series_title: Unemployment Compensation Review Commission Personnel File

series_description: Each work unit of Redeterminations and Appeals submits a production report on a weekly basis to their section assistant director. The report includes, but is not limited to, the number of appeals, overpayments, number of decisions received from the Review Commission, claims worked, claims completed, and work backlog. The totals and averages from these reports are used to produce quarterly reports of claims activity and the quarterly report totals and averages are tallied to produce a yearly report of operation production by the section.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 2 years ; Electronic: Retain in office 5 years, thendestroy.

series_description: Form GO2712 is used as a cover sheet for assignments completed by field audit staff. All documentation pertaining to the completed assignment is attached to the cover sheet. The name of staff person handling the assignment and the date the assignment was completed is also listed.

File may contain, but is not limited to, field audit assignment correspondence, report to determine liability, rate determinations, lien notices, notices of failure to file, quarterly delinquent notice of employer account, disposition/acquisition of business forms, audits unallocated payment information authorizations, address/actuary authorization, assessment notice, disposition of business, audit questionnaire, refund claims and account information. Employer Resource Information Center (ERIC)/Filenet.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until enterprise for which account was established is no longer in operation and its account has not been chargeable for a period of seven years and there are no outstanding debts ; Microform: Retain until enterprise for which account was established is no longer in operation and its account has not been chargeable for a period of seven years and there are no outstanding debts ; Paper: Retain 60 days after hardcopy is prepped, scanned (imaged), and quality control is complete
disposition: Electronic: Erase ; Microform: Shred ; Paper: Shred

series_description: File contains, but is not limited to, information and documentation related to audits of Unemployment Insurance(UI) Data Validation (DV) functions to ensure the State of Ohio is in compliance with federally mandated procedures (ETA 361 Benefits, 361 Tax, 412 UI DV Monitoring Guide, and 401 UI Reports Handbooks and ET Operations Guide 411) established for UI reporting. Monitoring reviews, including pre and post monitoring conferences to explain the scope of the review and any findings that occur, are conducted every three years of random samples of up to 28 components Ohio Job Insurance (OJI) Benefits populations 1, 2, 3, 3a, 4, 5, 12, 13, 14, Module 4, separation and non-separation quality; RC Express Benefits populations 6 – 11; Module 4, appeals quality; and Tax populations 1 – 5; Module 4 (Status New, Status Successor, Status Terminations/Inactivation’s, and Field Audits; Module 5, Wage Item Validation) for specific review periods. Department of Labor (DOL) conducts a regional review every three years.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 60 days after hardcopy is prepped, scanned (imaged), and quality control is complete ; Electronic: Retain in office for 4 years

series_description: Each ODJFS owned security camera(installed in or around ODJFS owned/leased office buildings)is wired to a Digital Video Recorder(DVR). The footage produced by the cameras for security purpose will be maintained on the DVR. The footage will not be reproduced after it is erased from the DVR. If needed/requested, the footage may be burned to a disk to saved for additional time as long as notification is made during the retention period. Footage burned to a disk will be distributed to the requestor, and will be retained by the requester, not by ODJFS Security.

series_description: Files contain weekly and/or monthly activity reports detailing measurable services provided to customers. These statistical measures are used to quantify Department of Labor (DOL) budget targets and agency performance as it relates to workload assumptions, to include time lapse for the Tier I and Tier II measures, and quality service performance measurements. Included, but not limited to statistical data collection for daily, weekly, monthly and annual statistical accounting of workload is collected on a daily basis. Reports that result from this data collection include: M5-130 report, quality appraisal, weekly status report (cases docketed, cased pending, average pendency, cased scheduled and timelines related to cases heard, reassigned, withdrawn, or dismissed, 1st and 2nd level appeal report, cases docketed and decision reports.

series_title: Lower & Higher Authority Appeal Case Files (including federal programs)
series_description: Files contain appeals from director's decisions and/or hearing officer decisions. The case files contain but is not limited to, one or more of the following: original appeal, any subsequent appeals including hearing officer, request for review, and court levels, exhibits, appeal and hearing notices, correspondences, docket information, decision(s). Case may involve unemployment compensation (UC) claims, any UC appeals, further appeals, tax liability, federal programs, mass or labor dispute, delinquent accounts or employer contribution rates. Case involving federal programs include: Federal Extended and Disaster Benefit program, UC-Ex-Military Personnel, Disaster Unemployment Assistance (DUA), Trade Readjustment Act (TRA), Federal Employees (UCFE), UC Federal Supplemental (USFSC), and any other new federal program involving unemployment claims.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 5 years after the case is closed and the decision if final ; Electronic: Retain for 5 years (to include federal programs) after the case is closed and the decisions is final after all appeals have been exhausted

retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office 5 years ; Paper: Retain in office 5 years

series_description: Manually generated form that contains eligibility criteria for Extended Unemployment Compensation(EUC)and regular Unemployment Compensation. Staff enter claimant''s personal information(e.g., name and address), the weekly benefit amount and affected "balance" of each program, program phase-out date and last week claimant may draw from the balance. the claimant is instructed to select a choice(i.e., to receive EUC or regular UI(Unemployment Insurance benefits) and respond within five business days by phone, mail, or fax dedicated folder. The hard copy form is then recycled.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until imaged ; Electronic: Retain in office 5 years

series_description: Record of credit card transactions. May include invoices, receipts, and payment card log which lists the purchase date, vendor name, item description, section or unit head, dollar amount, date received and date entered into the central accounting system. Also included in these files is e-mail correspondence related to the expenditures.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office for 1 fiscal year ; Paper: Retain in office for 1 fiscal year

series_description: Notification and reports documenting a disaster unemployment assistance event. File includes DUA declaration from the President of the United States, news release from the Governor, newspaper articles regarding the event, state in which the event occurred, nature of event (e.g., ice storm, flooding, landslide). File also contains weekly reports which identify the disaster number and event date, state program costs, administrative costs, number of applications received, number of weeks paid, number of benefits paid, number of appeals received, pending and resolved and closeout reports which summarize the event, claims activity, issues and recommendations during the DUA period.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 3 years after final action resolving the DUA event ; Electronic: Retain in office 3 years after final action resolving the DUA event

series_description: Special claims with base period employers that are filed in Ohio and one or more surrounding states. Claims can be a combination of an Ohio employer and an employer from another state, for military separation (UCX),federal employment (UCFE), (UCDA). Files include requests for wage information (IB-4) from employers, claimants, other state agencies and military entities, and responses to those requests (IB-5). ODJFS agency employee claims.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 1 year and 4 years in the records center are completed and released, and all discrepancies are resolved. ; Electronic: Retain until superseded, obsolete, or replaced

series_description: The Trade Adjustment Assistance (TAA) program helps re-employ workers who have lost their jobs due to international trade. Once the Petition is certified, the list of the workers must be maintained indefinitely; TAA is a lifetime benefit. Records included in this series would be copies of company petitions, personal information on the workers, BRI (Benefits Right Information) dates and training information for the workers.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 30 days after hardcopy is prepped, scanned (imaged), and quality control is complete ; Electronic: Retain permanently.

series_description: Trade job search and relocation contracts, expenses and payments.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain electronic documents for 4 years ; Paper: Retain 30 days after hardcopy is prepped, scanned (imaged), and quality control is complete

series_description: Claimant information file used for determining eligibility for unemployment compensation benefits under the Trade Readjustment Assistance Program. File contains claimant’s personal information and work history, application for benefits, notice of benefit determination, statement of reason for separation from employment, request for information from employer and employer responses, redetermination requests, interview notices and weekly claims for benefits.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 6 months past the last benefit payment and in the Records Center 5 years after benefit year ends provided no administrative or legal action is pending.

series_description: File contains written report of findings of telephone interviews with claimants, information faxed in by claimants, base period employer payroll and local office data, dependency documents and work search location information random audits of paid and denied claims. These samples are selected weekly from a random selection program in the SUN system, a federal system containing all claims information, operated by the Department of Labor.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 5 year, image and destroy. ; Electronic: Retain in the office 5 years.

series_description: Interagency information sharing agreements which are legal agreements/contracts between the Ohio Bureau of Employment Services and various county, state and federal agencies for the purpose of sharing confidential wage, claim, employer, and/or employment and training information.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 3 years after expiration of the agreement and 2 years in the records center ; Electronic: Retain draftagreement/contacts until nolonger of administrative value

series_description: Department prepares approvals (for Administrator) of requests from claimants who are receiving UC benefits, but who wish to attend school full-time and will not be looking for work. The training, which must be vocational in nature, must lead to a job when completed. File includes background research materials and school course outlines. Information is put on the computer network so it can be viewed by local office staff.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years ; Electronic: Retain 3 years

series_description: File contains score sheets and appraisal reviews of the quality of unemployment insurance performance in Ohio, based on performance standards set by the Department of Labor quarterly review focus on non-monetary operations based on allowed and denied claims, separation and non-separation issues.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office 5 years ; Paper: Retain in office 5 years

series_description: File contains, but is not limited to, list of attendees, agendas, handouts, presentations to employers on tax claims and special programs activity, updates regarding tax rate changes, extended benefits and Ohio Job Insurance changes.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 2 years

series_description: Spreadsheet of daily activity of the unemployment cash management fund. Spreadsheet tracks the daily deposits and withdrawals from the fund, and computes new balances and interest earned on the balances.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office area until audited by Auditor of State ,audit report is released, and all discrepancies are resolved

series_description: This record is a quarterly production report which is based upon data of Ohio employers and sent to the Department of Labor. It is referred to as the 581 Report. Information collected from the Tax Sections include, but is not limited to, total receivables for employers, timely reporting by employers, new business established and audit activity. The Bureau of UC Tax furnishes the data that the goes into the original report forwarded to LMI who sends it to DOL. Copies of the report go to all UC Tax sections.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 2 years

series_description: Labor disputes involving work stoppage, because of strike or lockout. Relevant to determine benefit eligibility. Files include: informal report (BEN 194), completed by local offices regarding possible labor disputes; labor dispute questionnaires (UC-440 and UC-441), completed by companies and unions involved in labor disputes; claimant lists; hearing transcripts; labor dispute determinations; and intra-agency correspondence regarding labor disputes.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in the office 1 years from the end of labor dispute ; Electronic: Retain electronic copies for 2 years

series_description: Records contain training agreement and supporting documentation necessary to substantiate applicant eligibility for federal training funds. File contains personal identifiable information.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain electronic documents for 4 years ; Paper: Retain 30 days after hardcopy is prepped, scanned (imaged), and quality control is complete

series_description: Record contains copies of Proof of Claim (POC) documentation received from the federal bankruptcy courts/attorneys where a claimant has filed for bankruptcy. Documentation includes claimant’s name, social security number, amount of payment and interest, if applicable, and the name of the court in which the bankruptcy was filed.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 6 years or until paid in full and no debt exists

series_description: File consists of spreadsheets recording daily activity of all UC accounts maintained by the cashier: e.g., General Ledgers of the Clearing, Special Administrative, Surcharge Trust Fund, Refund Warrant Registers and Cash Receipts Register, Journal Vouchers, Certificates of Deposit records, Dishonored Warrant records, and Daily Deposit summaries. Reports detail debits/credits to accounts by employer number, account number and general amount, daily deposits, benefit warrants, trust fund balances, and related correspondence.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until superseded, obsolete or replaced, thendestroy. ; Paper: Retain in office 4 years

series_description: File contains interoffice communications from the UC Trade Office requesting a special warrant be cut and issued to claimants to cover job search and relocation assistance expenses. Also included is Finance’s journal of entries which are used for an end of the month report.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 4 years

series_description: Reimbursements (e.g., checks, money orders, etc.) received from customers for benefit overpayments are delivered to ODJFS’ Lockbox #836 at National City Bank on a daily basis. On the following workday, the copies of the money items are picked up at the bank and delivered to Benefit Payments Finance for balancing. Subsequently, a report is sent to Treasurer of State stating the amount that has been deposited. The information is then entered into the Benefits System crediting claimants for monies paid.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until reimbursements are received and receipts are balanced daily against fund accounts ; Electronic: Retain in office until audited by Auditor of State, audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved

series_description: Statement of benefits paid to combined wage claimants which is sent to participating states on a quarterly basis. Each statement contains a list of claimants whose out of state wages have been used to establish Ohio eligibility. Each statement indicates the amounts of benefits Ohio is charging the participating state per quarter for its participation of Ohio combined wage claims, and itemizes the amount Ohio charges for each claimant listed on the statement. A “ledger record” is created in FoxPro for tracking purposes and to balance receipts against disbursements.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in the office for 4 years ; Electronic: Retain in the office for 4 years

series_title: Unemployment Compensation Benefits Payment Warrant Register
series_description: This file is a daily detailed listing (generated by MIS) of warrants cut on the previous day for payment of UC benefits. Information includes, but is not limited to, claimant’s name, social security number, fund monies taken from, amount of warrant, warrant number, date warrant issued and listing of canceled warrants. Treasurer of State images the warrants and the hard copy checks are sent to the records center to be retained for 90 days. A listing of these warrants, including the claimant’s name, SS#, warrant# and amount is also available on- line at their web-site via the Internet.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain warrant registers in office 4 years provided audited by Auditor of State, audit report released and all discrepancies are resolved. Destroy canceled/cleared warrant 90 days after received back from State Treasurer of Ohio. ; Microform: Retain 5 years, series no longer filmed.

series_description: File contains, but is not limited to, monthly bank reconciliation of the various Unemployment Compensation funds (e.g., UCFE, TEUC,DUA, UCX, Trade, NAFTA,) showing checks cleared, checks outstanding, dishonored checks and account balances. File also contains monthly activity book of all outstanding warrants, and ledgers identifying balances remaining in state and federal accounts, which are running balances of daily totals of deposit and disbursements, including UC’s account balance and bank balance which must match.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office 4 years

series_description: File contains a detailed spreadsheet of monies returned to ODJFS as a result of benefit overpayments sent to claimants in error. Information includes claimant's name, amount of overpayment and date received. A UC-322 reimbursement voucher is sent to the Treasurer of State for deposit into UC fund accounts.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office 4 years

series_description: Record includes test tape results, rejection letters, and other documents related to magnetic tape transmitter.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 4 months after hardcopy is prepped, scanned (imaged), and quality control is complete ; Electronic: Retain on system until enterprise for which the account was established is no longer in operation and its account has not been chargeable for a period of 7 years and there are no outstanding debts until printed.

series_description: Records contain quarterly employer wage details, e.g. social security number, name, wage and weeks worked. Which are used to determine eligibilit for unemployment compensation and cross-matching against other state and federal agency wage activity.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office seven 7 years ; Microform: Retain in office 5 years following the quarter microfilmed. Send the microfilm to the Records Center for an additional 2 years ; Paper: Retain in office 3 months and until microfilmed
disposition: Electronic: Erase ; Microform: Shred ; Paper: Shred

series_title: Reimbursing Negotiable Security and Surety Bond Files
series_description: File contains, but is not limited to, Reimbursing Employer Application, Treasurer of State receipts and release correspondence, inventory of securities with Treasurer of State, Reimbursing Employer Surety Bonds and related Continuation Certificates, Riders, Power of Attorney and related correspondence.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office for 2.5 years of inactivity subsequent to the effective discontinuance date and upon the proper disposition of all debits and the security instrument, image and certify ; Electronic: Retain in the System until enterprise for which account was established is no longer in operation and its account has not been chargeable for a period of 7 years, and there are no outstanding debts

series_description: File contains receipts booklets that are records of the monies collected by Compliance examiners and auditors from employers. Files are subject to be audited. These receipts booklets are maintained and tracked within the Compliance Receipt Log.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 6 years and

series_description: File contains specific policy and procedures information and communication s relevant to the duties of Compliance auditors and the overall operations of the field offices. Auditors take a copy with them when visiting employers to audit employer accounts to ensure compliance with tax laws.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until superceded, obsoleted or replaced

series_description: This record documents, on a monthly basis, completed assignments by Compliance audit staff in the agency’s eight districts. Report contains, but is not limited to, number and type of assignment per auditor, employer data, number of audit findings, monies collected and the number and amount of delinquencies. This statistical data is complied and merged into a report identifying staff production levels per month.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in the office 3 years ; Electronic: Retain in the office 3 years

series_description: These are records of ODJFS' administrative rules that govern the interpretation of our state statutes. They are primarily maintained electronically.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 60 days after hardcopy is prepped, scanned (imaged), and quality control is complete ; Electronic: Retain seven years.

series_description: Files include court docket papers, correspondence, and legal research for litigation involving ODJFS.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until closed

series_description: Proposed changes and additions to Social Services Programs, submitted to federal government for approval. Includes documentation on reviews and approvals by state officials, program descriptions, fiscal information, legal citations.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 3 years

series_description: Recipient appeals of state hearing decisions requested by the counties. Includes decision summaries, rulings, correspondence and legal research pertaining to the appeal.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 4 years ; Paper: Retain in office 90 days after prepped, imaged, indexed, and quality control of has been completed

series_description: Record of Hearing findings. Files include decisions written by state hearing officer, identifying information, record of events and persons involved, statement of the issues, summarized statement of facts, conclusions, citation of agency regulations, and final administrative decision and order. Also may contain Statistical Data for Federal Report SSA 4105 (DHS-4004) and State Hearing Decision form (DHS-4005). Administrative Appeal Decisions, County Review decisions, and any correspondence generated from HATS by hearing officer and county review decisions.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office, 4, years

series_description: Record contains documents used for individual state hearings. File may contain, but is not limited to, State Hearing Request form (DHS-4069), State Hearing Scheduling Notice (DHS-4002), Notice of Action taken on Hearing Request (DHS-4000), Hearing Record Certification (DHS-4015) and Appeals Summary (DHS-4067), Administrative Appeal Requests, County Reviews, Administrative Appeal Decisions, Compliance (JFS 04068), Compliance Records, Judicial Review Requests and Judicial Review Decisions.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until Records Automation has digitally scanned to compact disc. Destroy hard copy 60 days after prep, imaged, index and quality control is completed ; Electronic: Retain for 4 years

series_description: Record contains documents used for individual state hearings. File may contain, but is not limited to, State Hearing Request form (DHS-4069), State Hearing Scheduling Notice (DHS-4002), Notice of Action taken on Hearing Request (DHS-4000), Hearing Record Certification (DHS-4015) and Appeals Summary (DHS-4067), Administrative Appeal Requests, County Reviews, Administrative Appeal Decisions, Compliance (JFS 04068), Compliance Records, Judicial Review Requests and Judicial Review Decisions.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until RecordsAutomation has digitally scanned to compact disc.Destroy hard copy 60 days after prep, imaged, index and quality control is completed ; Electronic: Retain for 7 years

series_description: State Hearing Decision documentation that have had confidential information redacted. These records are used to respond to public inquiries.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain office 60 days after prepped, imaged, indexed, and quality contral of has been completed ; Electronic: Retain in office 4 years

series_description: Monthly hearing officer production reports sent to directors concerning the number of hearings conducted and requested, pending appeals, and decisions issued. Also included is a monthly report of cases that were not resolved in a timely manner, as required by the Peppers Decision.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office 4 years

series_description: Work papers for annual report sent to the federal Health and Human Services Department, which includes information on hearing disposition related to Medicaid, Temporary Assistance to Needy Families, and Supplemental Nutritional Assistance Programs. Also, work papers for annual report FNS 366-B sent to the Department of Agriculture, which concerns food assistance hearings and intentional program violations.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 90 days after prepped, imaged, indexed, and quality control of has been completed ; Electronic: Retain for 7 years

series_description: Request (JFS-04069) for a state hearing to appeal ODJFS action denying assistance to an individual. Documentation identifies appellant, appellant's address, program, county, dates action issued, and action appeal. Hearing request used for data entry into CRISE, Client Registry Information System Enhanced.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain paper documents 60 days after prepped, imaged, indexed, and quality contral of has been completed ; Electronic: Retain 7 years

series_description: Used to record hearing decisions on program violations by a recipient. Hearings are requested by counties.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 90 days after hardcopy is prepped, scanned (imaged), and quality control is complete ; Electronic: Retain 7 years

series_description: State Hearing Decision documentation that have had confidential information redacted. These records are used to respond to public inquiries.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 4 years ; Paper: Retain in office 60 days after hardcopy is prepped, scanned (imaged), and quality control is complete

series_description: Materials related to the submission of the OCSE-34A (Part 1) Quarterly Report of Collections; OCSE 34-A (Part 2) Itemized Undistributed Collections used to track services under the Child Support program. This report is completed and filed quarterly as a preliminary report with revisions possible within the quarter filed.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 2 years and 3 years in the records center ; Electronic: Retain until superseded, obsolete, or replaced

series_description: Materials related to the submission of the OCSE 157 Annual Performance Report created to track services under the Child Support program. This report is completed and filed annually as a preliminary report with revisions possible prior to the end of the calendar year.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until superseded, obsolete, or replaced ; Paper: Retain in office 2 years and 3 years in the records center

series_title: Child Support County Monitoring Review Files
series_description: Records contain documents pertaining to review of county child support enforcement agencies to ensure performance and corrective action plans are in in compliance with state and federal regulations governing the child support program. Files include monitoring reports, case review documentation, audit notes and work papers, minutes of review sessions, and related correspondence.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 1 year in office, 2 years in State Records Center ; Electronic: Retain 3 years provided state and federal audits are released and all discrepancies are resolved.

series_description: Claimant information file used for determining eligibility for unemployment compensationbenefits. It contains personal information on claimant & claimant''s work history. This unemployment compensation filecontains confidential information and documentation regarding initial, continued, and additional claims forunemployment benefits.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain paper documents 60 days afterprepped, imaged, indexed , and qualitycontrol performed and completed, thendestroy. ; Electronic: Retain in office 1 benefit year,records center 4 years

series_description: County requests for Reduction of Permanently Assigned Arrears Waivers and/or Compromises received by the Office of Child Support.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office 5 years.

series_title: Disaster Plan Operating Manuals for the State of Ohio
series_description: Records include procedures and emergency contacts for resuming child support programs in the event of a disaster.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until superseded, obsolete, or replaced

series_description: Records contain documentation regarding state and federal audits of the child support program, and related e-mail correspondence in response to findings.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 2 years and 3 years in the records center ; Electronic: Retain 5 years

series_description: Records contain Full Time Equivalent (FTE) reports submitted by all 88 Ohio counties, using the JFS 04234. The reports are used by the Ohio Department of Job and Family Services (ODJFS) to complete part of the federal OCSE-157, which is an annual report based on the federal fiscal year (October 1 through September 30) and submitted to the U. S. Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) at the end of the federal fiscal year.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 2 years and 3 years in the records center ; Electronic: Retain until superseded, obsolete or replaced

series_title: Child Support State Plan and Amendments
series_description: Records contain a detailed plan, describing the nature and scope of the Ohio Child Support program, submitted to the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Administration for Children and Families. Approval for federal financial participation is contingent on the plan's conformance with title IV -D requirements. All changes are re-submitted for federal approval. Includes documentation on reviews and approvals by state officials, program descriptions, fiscal information, legal citations, along with organizational charts and staffing levels.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 5 year and review by State . ; Electronic: Retain until superseded orobsolete

series_description: Records contain federal grants applications to provide for access/visitation services in the county offices. Includes proposal, grant agreements, allocation letters, financial information, and quarterly activity reports monitoring compliance.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 3 years ; Paper: Retain in office 1 year, store in records center for 2 years

series_description: Records contain 3123.95, including JFS forms 7021, JFS 07022, and JFS 07023 which are related to the child support poster program under Ohio Revised Code section.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 5 years ; Electronic: Retain until superseded, obsolete, or replaced

series_description: Records include petitions to establish child support cases on behalf of other states under the Uniform Interstate Family Support Act (UIFSA) and status inquiries for processing interstate child support cases. Information is entered into the Statewide Enforcement Tracking System (SETS) and then the records are sent to the county agency responsible for administering the case.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 30-days after imaging and ; Electronic: Retain 1 year

series_description: Reciprocal agreements for child support purposes between Ohio and the governments of other countries.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until agreements are superseded, obsolete or replaced. ; Electronic: Retain in office until agreements are superseded, obsolete or replaced.

series_description: Records include bank statements for all 88 Ohio counties and other various accounts, showing all banking activity in child support transactions, such as, disbursements, collections, adjustments and returned deposit items (RDI).
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 3 years and 2 years in records center, provided state and federal audits are completed, audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved. ; Electronic: Retain 5 years

series_title: Child Support Payment Central Project File
series_description: Records include documentation of the development and ongoing management of the child support payment central project. Includes project proposals and bid documentation, a copy of the current contract for processing support payments, any amendments to the contract, copies of invoices, planning documents and related correspondence.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 5 years ; Paper: Retain in office 2 years and 3 years in records center. Destroy provided state and federal audits are complete, audit reports are released and all discrepancies are resolved. Also, review by State for possible retention or .
disposition: Electronic: Erase ; Paper: Shred Destruction Archives

series_description: Records contain documentation related to direct deposit such as: debit card enrollment (e.g. enrollment applications), client correspondence and responses, and Notice of Change (NOC) documentation).
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 90 days after hardcopy is prepped, scanned (imaged), and quality control is complete ; Electronic: Retain in office 2 years and

series_description: Records contain bank statements for all 88 Ohio counties and other various accounts, showing all banking activity in child support transactions, such as, disbursements, collections, adjustments, and Returned Deposit Items (RDI).
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 3 years and 5 years in records center, provided state and federal audits are completed, audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved. ; Electronic: Retain 7 years

series_description: System project files containing proposals, cost figures,award letters, meeting minutes and related correspondence for developing the ChildSupport Enforcement Tracking System. Also, contains system procedures, maintenanceand testing data, report scripts, job control language, and master tape procedures.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until the system is superseded, obsolete or replaced ; Electronic: Retain until the system is superseded, obsolete or replaced

series_description: Record documents electronic movement of monies from the bank (Star Bank , Fifth-Third, Bank One) to the various accounts (e.g. Master, Recoupment, and Suspense). File contains, but is not limited to, reports from the bank that money is being moved to a specific account, vendor (Affiliated Computer Services ACS) report of transaction activity, transfer history detail reports, Treasurer of State Account disbursement report, Excel and Quattro Pro disbursement spreadsheets, SETS allocated and unallocated report printouts, and related correspondence.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 1 year and 5 years in records center, image ; Electronic: Retain in office 6 years

series_description: Records include documents from each Ohio county (88) to account for and reconcile all financial data relating to the child support transactions of a county’s activity. The ledgers are created from information contained in Support Enforcement Tracking System (SETS) daily allocation financial management reports. Each file includes, but is not limited to, collection and disbursement reports (allocated and unallocated funds); financial corrections data; CMR data; SETS reports with spreadsheets and supporting documents; copies of bank statements and bank transaction adjustments; and daily non-standard activity (NSA) logs, as well as other various supporting documentation from SETS.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 3 years in office until state and federal audits are completed, audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved. ; Paper: Paper: Retain in office 1 years and 2 years in records center

series_description: Responses to customer inquiries regarding Child Support program services and case disposition. Inquiries can be by telephone (call center tallies), paper or via electronic transmission. Information may include name, address, county and state, obligor, requester and nature of inquiry.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 1 year, 4 years in records center Retain printed reports until no longer of adminstrative value, then destroy. ; Electronic: Retain until superseded, obsolete or replaced

series_description: Records contain budget documents related to annual expenditures for personnel and benefits, equipment, services (includes personal contracts, training, and temporary staffing), and maintenance (includes, supplies, travel, telecommunication costs). Records detailing budget narratives, expenditure justification, and cost breakdowns showing how appropriations are allocated. Files include executive budget proposals; internal budget plans and projections; bid proposals; copies of purchase orders and invoices; reports and supportive documentation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 2 years after the end of the biennium and in records center 3 years ; Electronic: in office until superseded, obsolete or replaced

series_description: Record supports adjustment transactions made as a result of research and analysis to correct payments to child support cases in SETS. Files include, but are not limited to, deposit errors, overpayments, misapplied disbursements made to the wrong case or incorrectly applied to a case, unprocessed transactions due to missing or invalid information, and items received that either don't contain enough information to properly post to a child support case or are received from another state that can not be posted in Ohio. Research documentation is used to analyze and determine the best course of corrective action to resolve each item.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office 5 years ; Paper: Retain in office 5 years

series_description: County Child Support Enforcement Agency agreements with government and non-government providers for child support services that are reviewed by Fiscal Administration to ensure compliance with the Child Support Program, and rejected or accepted under those guidelines. The agreements can run from 3 months to 1 year. Providers agree to deliver services to establish and enforce child support obligations, collect support, and cooperate with other states to enforce e.g. genetic testing, credit checks, paternity searches, and legal services through the court system. The agreements are assembled and submitted in a format prescribed in OAC
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 2 years, store in record's center for 5 years ; Electronic: Retain for 7 years

series_title: Child Support Payment Central Collection Documentation
series_description: Records contain remittance information from remitters (e.g. employers, obligors, financial institutions, Unemployment Compensation, Bureau of Worker’s Compensation, etc.) that are received by Child Support Payment Central(CSPC) with payments for child support obligations. Records may contain the obligor’s name, social security number, case number, payment amount and related child support case information.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 60 days after hardcopy is prepped, scanned (imaged), and quality control is complete ; Electronic: Retain for 7 years

series_description: Child Support Enforcement tracking and reporting system (SETS)devised to document attempts to obtain adequate child support services. Services include establishingpaternity, locating an absent parent, tracking child support payments, collecting licensesuspension/revocation and new hire reporting data which is used to enforce child support obligations,and absent parent data reported to the IRS to intercept tax refunds.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain reports printed from system until superseded, obsolete or replaced ; Electronic: Retain SETS data in system for 7 years

series_title: Statewide Enforcement Tracking System(SETS) Public Assistance Refunds
series_description: Records documenting Public Assistance payments that were sent to the State in error. The SETS/PA Refund process was created to reimburse clients for the overpayment of child support collections sent to ODJFS.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 2 years and 5 years in Records Center

series_description: Files contain requests from child support consumers/customers to have their PIN''s reset in the interactive voice response (IVR) system in order to check on status of case disposition or child support warrant. The resets are usually requested due to forgotten or compromised PIN''''''''s. The requests contain the nature of the request, a picture ID or SS# and must be signed and dated. Data is entered into Remedy for tracking purposes and SETS as proof of reset.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 1 year ; Electronic: Retain until superseded, obsolete or replaced

series_description: Records contain inquiries from customers, along with any supporting documentation, and CICC’s responses to those inquiries. Inquiries are received via telephone, paper or electronic transmission. Information may include customer’s Personal Identifiable Information (PII), confidential case information, and the nature of the inquiry.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 1 year, 4 years in records center destroy. ; Electronic: Retain 5 years

series_description: Records contain Federal Tax Information (FTI ) safeguarding files, including copies of internal inspection reports, self inspection reports provided by counties, email correspondence, Plan of Action, Milestones (POAM), IRS Corrective Action Plans (CAP), Safeguarding Activity Reports (SAR), and Safeguarding Procedures Reports (SPR)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office until superseded, obsolete, or replaced ; Electronic: Retain in office until superseded, obsolete, or replaced

series_description: Record contains JFS 07014, Tax Information Safeguarding Authorization Agreement, signed by agency employees certifying employee understands the agency’s security policy and procedures for Safeguarding vital IRS information. This signed form grants employees access to federal tax information. Record also contains JFS 07072, Safeguarding of Internal Revenue Service, Ohio Department of Taxation, Federal Parent Locator Service, and Unemployment Compensation Information form signed by applicable county Child Support Enforcement Agency director.

series_description: Records document transactions in the Support Enforcement Tracking System (SETS) that cause the release of monies affecting funds on child support cases. CMR (Canceled Unclaimed Funds or CUF/Manual Checks/Recoupments) either initiates or approves county requests for financial corrections or manual checks. Requests can be rejected because of invalid data or lack of funding in the master account. Transactions may reconcile and correct payments made in error or initiate replacement checks for checks that have been voided or payment has been stopped for certain reasons. Files which are stored in databases may contain, but are not limited to, financial corrections, payment corrections, manual check requests, and comments. Other supporting records include Adjudicated Arrears Initiative (AAI), related correspondence, workflows, manuals and special projects. Requests are tracked within the CMR Access Database.

The regional or local plan must support the strategy described in Ohio's Combined State Plan and represents a comprehensive, customer-focused, and actionable service delivery strategy.

series_description: Child Advocacy Center(CAC) Start-Up files include copies of request for applications, grant applications, grant agreements, scoring tools, award letters, progress reports, and related e-mail and correspondence.

series_title: Migrant Seasonal Farm Worker Activity Reports
series_description: Quarterly and annual reports of One Stop office Migrant Outreach staff employment service delivery activities, such as worker registrations, referrals, placements and other supportive services, including web site activity detailing job openings. File also includes documentation regarding worker concerns and problems surrounding these activities, and recommendations to resolve same, as well as a log of complaints received. Reports are sent to the Department of Labor (DOL)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 3 years ; Electronic: Retain in office 3 years

series_description: Documentation to determine tax credit eligibility under the Work Opportunity Tax Credit (WOTC) program for certified applications.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 1 year and in records center 4 years ; Electronic: Retain until superseded, obsolete or replaced

series_description: Record details about the development of Ohio’s plan for creating Ohio Means Jobs Center in local workforce development areas. Ohio Means Jobs Centers are designed to provide training, counseling and employment services to Ohio job seekers and employers under the Wagner-Peyser, Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (WIOA) and Labor Exchange (LE) programs. Services must address employer needs, be customer focused and accountable. Files should include: grant information, Memorandum of understanding, partner agreements, cost sharing process, allocation plan and all WIOA related correspondence.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years after the submittal of the final closeout expenditure report for that funding period and any audits are completed, audit reports are released and all discrepancies are resolved. ; Electronic: Retain 3 years after the submittal of the final closeout expenditure report for that funding period and any audits are completed, audit reports are released and all discrepancies are resolved

series_description: Ohio's Apprenticeship Program files for apprenticeship participants. These are contracts between sponsors and apprentices to confirm eligibility and participation in program. These files are used for compliance reviews.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain electronically permanent.

series_description: Ohio’s Apprenticeship Program provides career opportunities that combine educational instruction with hands-on job training to provide certification and employment in industry sectors such as manufacturing, information technology, social services, health and support services, and aerospace, etc. Files include grant applications, curriculum, correspondence of compliance and quality reviews, active apprenticeship agreements, and all change in status transmittals until such time as the standards or apprenticeship agreements are canceled.
retention_statement: Paper: ;Retain in office 5 years after the program ends and until audits are completed, audit reports are released and all discrepancies are resolved. ; Electronic: Retain in office 5 years after the program ends and until audits are completed, audit reports are released and all discrepancies are resolved.

series_description: Records contain supporting documentation such as training, and funding applications with possible employers FEIN numbers as a part of the Rapid Response applications.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office 3 years after the submittal of the final closeout expenditure report for that funding period. Maintain audits, and audit reports until they are released and all discrepancies are resolved.

series_title: Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (WIOA) Program Annual Reports
series_description: Annual publication of program and service activity mandated under the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (WIOA) program. Report may include a wide range of topics, e.g. message from the Governor, the state of Ohio's economy, human interest stories, financial information, program evaluation and performance accountability, Workforce Policy Board Initiatives, Workforce system information, data collection information and customer satisfaction outcomes. WIOA information is also drawn from the Ohio Workforce Case Management System.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years after the submittal of the final closeout expenditure report for that funding period or date of final audit resolution, view by state prior to disposal. ; Electronic: Retain 3 years after the submittal of the final closeout expenditure report for that funding period or date of final audit resolution, view by state archives prior to disposal.

series_title: Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act(WIOA) Combined State Plan
series_description: Under the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (WIOA), the Governor of each State must submit a Unified or Combined State Plan to the Secretary of the U.S. Department of Labor that outlines a four-year strategy for the State’s workforce development system. The publicly-funded workforce development system is a national network of Federal, State, regional, and local agencies and organizations that provide a range of employment, education, training, and related services and supports to help all job-seekers secure good jobs while providing businesses with the skilled workers they need to compete in the global economy.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office 5 years ; Paper: Retain in office 5 years

series_title: Work Opportunity Tax Credit Program (WOTC) Denials
series_description: Applications for Work Opportunity Tax Credit Program (WOTC)Tax Credits submitted by employers or employer representatives to obtain a federal tax credit which were denied after the review process. These applications may be paper or electronic format
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 1 year from denial date ; Electronic: Retain in office 1 year from denial date

series_title: OhioMeansJobs.com and Advancement of Resources through Information and Employment Services
series_description: OhioMeansJobs.com and the Advancement of Resources through Information and Employment Services (ARIES) are web-based systems that share career opportunity and job training information with respective employers, job seekers, and training providers under state and federal workforce programs. The system contains job order, job applicant, referral, matching, placement, and training information; a listing of training participants funded under WIOA; training information for youth, adult, and displaced workers; a list of eligible training providers; and related federal quarterly reports based on the above statistical information. This information is entered daily into the system by state and local workforce staff. ARIES also interfaces with a national listing of job order activity placed by employers and job seekers.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office 5 years

series_description: Record documents research conducted in support of the Ohio's Workforce Policy Board and local Workforce Investment boards. File includes, but is not limited to, research information on market analysis of key workforce trends for the State's economic development; high technology report; demographic labor force and industry trends; expected and or projected changes in the labor force based on research analysis.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until no longer of administrative value or until successor report issued

series_description: Records contain documentation associated with conducting the Occupational Employment and Wage Statistics (OEWS) survey. File includes, but is not limited to survey questionnaires and telephone contact sheets.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office 4 years ; Paper: Retain in office 4 years

Case files are referenced in instances where progressive discipline is being considered.

The minutes include, but is not limited to an agenda, meeting log, commission minutes and documents submitted during service improvement discussions. Discussion centers around case conflicts, customer service issues, and recommendations for improvements.

Requests are made as a result of changes in the law, operational need, and software improvements.

retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 3 years ; Electronic: Retain in office 3 years from the date of center closure

series_description: These records were generated for the 6-year project to evaluate the effectiveness of ODJFS’ Ohio Works First (OWF) program that provides time-limited cash assistance to needy families with (or expecting) children, and furnishes parents or specified relatives with work, training, and other support services they need in order to attain permanent self-sufficiency while meeting the family's on going basic needs. The records include: 1) individual surveys of recipient’s OWF experience, employment situation, wage information, health and health insurance status, access to health services, and general well being or barriers to that well being; 2) wage record, Medicaid, food stamp, housing and child care, eligibility, benefit and services data generated from CRIS-E, Benefits, and Data Warehouse systems; 3) mail survey data from 10 ODJFS county offices staff concerning their experiences and opinions about OWF and OWF clients; 4)summaries from on-site interviews with management staff in 10 counties to discuss their experiences with implementing the OWF program; and 5) various financial, contractual, correspondent, and project management information.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years in records center ; Electronic: Retain until no longer of administrativevalue

series_description: Monthly, quarterly, trimester, and annual reports compiled for the Federal government, ODJFS, and County agencies involving public assistance and social services programs for historical, management, and performance purposes. Programs include but are not limited to Ohio Works First (OWF), Child Care, DA Financial, Food Assistance, and Refugee Social Services reporting.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in record center for 10 years ,review by State before disposal. ; Electronic: Retain in office 10 years, thendestroy provided audits arecompleted, reports are releasedand all discrepancies areresolved.

series_description: Fraud Control follows up on case notices and letters generated by MIS from the Client Registry Information System (CRIS-E) data to the counties in regard to overpayments or incorrect payments to recipients of food stamps (federal) or cash assistance (state). Monies are recovered from client’s federal tax refunds or are refunded to clients when deducted incorrectly. The bulk of the hard copy files are maintained by the counties. File includes a spreadsheet which tracks the client’s name and address, date of refund, warrant number and amount.

series_description: The Ohio Training Tax Credit Program (OTTC) is a non-refundable franchise tax credit that is designed to encourage eligible Ohio employers to provide job skills and necessary training to those in Ohio's workforce who are at risk of being displaced because of skill deficiencies or the inability to use new technologies. File includes: application and authorization to disclose forms; payroll information; FEI and NAICS codes and copy of application fee; clearance documents from EPA and/or Ohio Department of Taxation; copies of denial letters; and related correspondence. Also includes information regarding the scope of training, participant and provider information, surveys about the course, training costs and receipts for monies spent on training.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 5 years following the end of the year in which the tax credit is claimed ; Electronic: Retain in office 5 years following the end of the year in which the tax credit is claimed

series_title: Re-employment (Profiling) Service Customer Satisfaction Survey and Lists
series_description: Re-employment Services is a program established by a 1993 federal law that requires that each person receiving UC benefits be entered into a filtering system to identify whether or not they were employed in a declining industry and will be likely to exhaust their benefits. The program provides early intervention, by offering re-employment assistance. The file contains surveys which ask questions pertaining to profiling and the surveys are sent to claimants upon completion of the program. Also contains weekly list of customers (claimants) eligible for re-employment services through the program.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 1 year and the Record Center 4 years

series_description: Customer Survey, Individual Reemployment Plan, Attendance Sheet, Job Contacts, Resumes, and weekly list of customers (claimants) eligible for reemployment services. series_description: Manual Reemployment Services assessment, Customer Survey, Individual Reemployment Plan, Acknowledgement Forms, Attendance Sheet, Job Contacts, Resumes, and weekly list of customers (claimants) eligible for reemployment services. series_description: The Equity Grant Language Translation Project addresses adding language options other than English to multiple self-serve options associated with multiple Unemployment Insurance (UI) platforms. The implementation of a Limited English Proficiency (LEP) tool on the Ohio Unemployment Insurance (UI) Help website enables LEP contact center constituents/users to access UI information in their native language to better understand options and how to apply for UI benefits. This solution provides a functionality to allow for real-time translations in multiple languages, thus allowing ODJFS to reach a larger audience regarding UI services. The records for this functionality include chat translation and Chat AI Bot (system program designed to simulate communication using a text interface and artificial intelligence [AI]) including question and answer (Q/A) transcripts, Interactive Voice Response (IVR) tree translation and Voice AI (artificial intelligence) Bot. The translation functionality provides two-way translation of chat conversation and translation of IVR tree allowing an LEP constituent/user to converse fluidly with a chat agent and navigate through the IVR tree in their native language. The Google translation used for any agency function will be supplemented with a Glossary developed by the Agency to ensure commonly used language and terms are in the correct context and have the appropriate meaning. Both sets of data will be stored in English and the selected language within the Google Cloud Platform (GPC) environment. The storage processes include redaction for Personally Identifiable Information (PII). The top ten languages for Ohio claimants are identified as: 1. Spanish 2. Somali 3. Arabic 4. Mandarin 5. French 6. Nepali 7. Amharic 8. Russian 9. Tigrinya 10. Vietnamese
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 5 years
